chunks.text: Page 1 of 3 ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No.D-1743 of 2025. DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For orders on office objections. For hearing of main case. 24.02.2026. Mr. Muntazir Mehdi Laghari, Advocate for Petitioners. Mr. Zulfiqar Ali Korai, Advocate for Respondent No.3 to 5 alongwith Zubair Ahmed Pathan (CFO_WASA). Mr. Naeem Uddin Sahito, Advocate for Respondent No.6 (HDA). Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, Additional Advocate General, Sindh. ***************************************** The petitioners have approached this Court seeking release of their service‐ related dues, including monthly pension, gratuity/commutation, LPR, leave encashment and arrears of salary. 2. Learned counsel for respondents No.3 to 5 is present along with the Chief Finance Officer, WASA, and has filed a detailed statement accompanied by a comprehensive payment schedule pertaining to all three petitioners. A copy of the same has been supplied to learned counsel for the petitioners, who expresses satisfaction but submits that the monthly pension and salary of the petitioners have not yet commenced. Upon this submission, learned counsel for respondents No.3 to 5, in the presence of the Chief Finance Officer, undertakes that the monthly pension of all petitioners shall commence from March 2026. 3. The payment schedule filed by the Chief Finance Officer reflects that in respect of Petitioner No.1 (Muhammad Akram), the outstanding monthly pension from July, 2024 to June, 2025 at the rate of Rs.154,218/- for twelve months and from October 2025 to January 2026 at the rate of Rs.165,301/- for four months total Rs.2,511,820/-.

Upon this submission, learned counsel for respondents No.3 to 5, in the presence of the Chief Finance Officer, undertakes that the monthly pension of all petitioners shall commence from March 2026. 3. The payment schedule filed by the Chief Finance Officer reflects that in respect of Petitioner No.1 (Muhammad Akram), the outstanding monthly pension from July, 2024 to June, 2025 at the rate of Rs.154,218/- for twelve months and from October 2025 to January 2026 at the rate of Rs.165,301/- for four months total Rs.2,511,820/-. This amount is to be paid in five equal monthly installments of Rs.502,364/- each, commencing from March, 2026 Page 2 of 3 and concluding in July, 2026. His commutation amount of Rs.4,654,910/- is to be paid in nine equal monthly installments of Rs.517,212/- each, beginning in August, 2026 and ending in April, 2027. 4. Regarding Petitioner No.2 (Muhammad Ishaque Siddique), the outstanding monthly pension from 6th February, 2025 to June, 2025, along with pension for October, 2025 to January, 2026, total Rs.1,010,506/-. This amount is to be paid in four equal monthly installments of Rs.252,627/- each from March, 2026 to June, 2026. His outstanding monthly salaries from August, 2024 to November, 2024 at Rs.248,160/- per month and from December 2024 to 5th February, 2025 at Rs.256,455/- total Rs.1,551,346/-. This amount is to be paid in five equal monthly installments of Rs.310,269/- each from July 2026 to December 2026. His commutation share payable by WASA/HW&SC amounts to Rs.3,606,062/-, which is to be paid in six equal monthly installments of Rs.601,010/- each from January, 2027 to June, 2027. His commutation share payable by HDA amounts to Rs.274,017/-, which HDA shall pay directly. His LPR amount of Rs.1,648,920/- is to be paid in six equal monthly installments of Rs.274,820/- each from July, 2027 to December, 2027. 5. As regards Petitioner No.3 (Muhammad Jawad Kamal), the outstanding monthly pension from 16th September, 2024 to June, 2025, along with pension from October, 2025 to January, 2026, total Rs.2,119,933/-. This amount is to be paid in seven equal monthly installments of Rs.302,847/- each from March, 2026 to September, 2026.

As regards Petitioner No.3 (Muhammad Jawad Kamal), the outstanding monthly pension from 16th September, 2024 to June, 2025, along with pension from October, 2025 to January, 2026, total Rs.2,119,933/-. This amount is to be paid in seven equal monthly installments of Rs.302,847/- each from March, 2026 to September, 2026. His outstanding monthly salaries from July, 2024 to 15th September, 2024 total Rs.667,195/-, which shall be paid in two equal installments of Rs.333,598/- each in October and November, 2026. His commutation share payable by WASA amounts to Rs.4,596,160/-, which is to be paid in nine equal monthly installments of Rs.510,684/- each from December, 2026 to August, 2027. His commutation share payable by HDA amounts to Rs.401,906/-, which HDA shall pay directly. His LPR amount of Rs.1,607,880/- is to be paid in four equal monthly installments of Rs.401,970/- each from September, 2027 to December, 2027. His G.P. Fund has already been paid. In view of the detailed schedule submitted and the undertaking that monthly pension shall commence from March, 2026, the instant petition along with pending application (s), stands disposed of with directions to Page 3 of 3 respondents No.3 to 5 to strictly comply with the payment schedule in its entirety, in letter and spirit. Any deviation, delay or non‐ compliance shall expose the responsible officials to contempt proceedings before this Court. JUDGE JUDGE Ali.
